success_factors: Workflow reached terminal status `success` and executed all planned nodes: download, analysis, env, generate, run, review, finalize.
Import-based adapter strategy was appropriate for astroquery’s class-centric API surface and low intrusiveness risk.
Repository structure was successfully parsed at scale (586 files discovered; 62 processed in workflow path).
Generated MCP service started healthy over stdio, indicating runnable scaffolding and valid entrypoint wiring.
failure_reasons: Quality signal gap: original project tests show `passed: false` with empty details and zero execution time, so upstream compatibility is unverified.
DeepWiki analysis failed, reducing external architectural/context enrichment.
Generation metrics show `total_lines_of_code: 0`, `generated_files_size: 0`, and `tool_endpoints: 0`, suggesting telemetry/reporting inconsistency or incomplete instrumentation.

node_performance.download_time: Completed successfully; repository imported via zip fallback. No per-node timing provided, but no download instability reported.
node_performance.analysis_time: Completed successfully on a large multi-package codebase; analysis quality is high despite DeepWiki fallback.
node_performance.generation_time: Generation node succeeded and output files were created, but code-size/endpoint counters are inconsistent with listed endpoints.
node_performance.test_time: MCP plugin health test passed; however, upstream/original test execution appears skipped or not captured.
